title: Fedora 31: podman failed with "Error: could not get runtime: unknown event logger type:"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot run podman `root-less` on Fedora 31with the following error:
```
Error: could not get runtime: unknown event logger type:
```
But podman can be run on `root`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podnam` on root-less user on Fedora 31

2. Failed with above error

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: could not get runtime: unknown event logger type:
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Run without error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Cannot run this command due to this issue. But `podman -v` can be run with result:
```
podman version 1.6.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Cannot run this command due to this issue.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
podman-manpages-1.6.2-2.fc31.noarch
```

comments.body: If you do not have any custom configuration in `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf` I recommend deleting the file; Podman should recreate one with sane defaults which will not have this issue.

Thanks @mheon! Your comment works for me.
`podman` works on my Fedora 30, but it failed after update to Fedora 31. But I did not find any related break changes for `podman` from Fedora 31 release doc. Only found one common bug, which is about docker, on [Common F31 bugs](https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Common_F31_bugs#Docker_package_no_longer_available_and_will_not_run_by_default_.28due_to_switch_to_cgroups_v2.29)
We'll see about getting this added to the Fedora upgrade issues list.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@mheon What do you want to do with this?
I think we may be able to resolve it. We want to do a 1.6.4 for Fedora anyways, so this would be a good opportunity. I'll look into it.
